theme: unicursal hedge labyrinth that looks fourfold
title: Veiled Fourfold Hedge
mutation: composite: the corridor must be a single unbranching entrance-to-center path near-covering the grid (hamiltonian-corridor-path), scored not by length but by a 3-way weighted objective coupling wall-set D4 symmetry defect, turn-sequence rhythm entropy, and depth-to-center oscillation -- and a corner entrance makes perfect symmetry provably infeasible (orbit-counting bound), so the optimal strategy must approach, not reach, that bound.
strategies: trivial: plain spiral-matrix traversal from entrance to center; no attempt at symmetry/rhythm/oscillation (checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)

naive greedy: patch the spiral with a bounded 2-opt local search that ONLY accepts moves raising turn-rhythm entropy, with no mechanism to protect or seek symmetry/oscillation; since a spiral's 2-opt slack is almost entirely ring-crossing, those two terms move as unplanned side effects and greedy fares far worse than strong whenever the input weights make them matter

insight (strong): reformulate as local search over the FULL joint weighted objective, using two structural facts -- (1) the entrance corner's D4 orbit makes symScore=1.0 provably unreachable, so the search should chase the reachable bound via direct orbit re-scoring, not blind symmetrization; (2) plain spiral depth is piecewise-constant per ring (oscScore=0 identically), so only ring-crossing 2-opt moves can earn oscillation credit -- optimizing the composite (not a single term) directly discovers and keeps exactly the moves that are net-positive across all three mechanisms
